message:
test(graph_utilities): compare non_edges by set instead of ordered list

``test_global_non_edges_matches_networkx_without_fallback`` was
intermittently flaking in the full suite for the DiGraph and
MultiDiGraph parametrizations. The failure was not the monkeypatched
"NetworkX utility fallback should not be used" signal — it was a
pure ordering mismatch:

    At index N diff: ('c', 'd') != ('c', 'b')

``non_edges`` is documented as returning an iterator, and internally
both nx and fnx compute it via set-difference — set iteration order
depends on process hash state and can legitimately diverge when
preceding tests (notably test_thread_safety concurrent workers) mutate
the internal adjacency structure's iteration order.

The intent of the test is "fnx did not fall through to nx.non_edges"
(already enforced by ``_block_networkx_utilities`` monkeypatch) and
"fnx yields the same set of non-edges". Compare by ``set`` and
``len`` to stay robust; the upstream contract never promised an
ordering, so matching by set covers the true invariant.

Verified: 15 consecutive full-suite runs now report 3876 passed, 0
failed (the xfail eigsh case oscillates harmlessly).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1131,7 +1131,15 @@ def test_global_non_edges_matches_networkx_without_fallback(
 
     _block_networkx_utilities(monkeypatch, "non_edges")
 
-    assert list(fnx.non_edges(graph)) == expected_result
+    actual_result = list(fnx.non_edges(graph))
+    # non_edges is specified as returning an iterator of edges; the
+    # ordering is set-iteration-order, which depends on the hash state
+    # of the process and can diverge between nx and fnx even when both
+    # yield the same set of pairs (observed when thread_safety tests
+    # precede this one). Compare by set to stay robust — the "no nx
+    # fallback" contract is already enforced by the monkeypatch.
+    assert set(actual_result) == set(expected_result)
+    assert len(actual_result) == len(expected_result)
 
 
 @pytest.mark.parametrize(
